we will be doing a better job of demonstrating it
as so when we, as we, uh, mature our practices
around information as we report in our annual report, um,
as we have that information also flowing more routinely
through our internal management reporting, um, uh,
but particularly it's closing the information loop.
That is the absolutely critical piece for that,
because that at the moment we can be,
so we have no complaints on whether we fail
to meet an RFR obligation.
We know the process is robust around disposals.
What I, what I can't do,
because we don't have the information system set up
to do it, is positively affirm.
So I have no reason to believe not, we need
to do a better job of being able to positively affirm.
Okay. Very good. Supplementary, please.
Um, you came into the role, um, Richard about a year ago.
Correct. And, uh, just out of curiosity,
when there was the handover from your predecessor to you,
um, did this,
did treaty obligations feature in the, in the conversation?
Uh, yes. Uh, but specifically in the,
and also in the fact that we had created a new team for it,
which at the time was, had, had no staff in it.
Um, and that my, one
of my first jobs coming into the role was to recruit the
RA Maori relationships, hence.
Thank you. Well, I think we're exhausted
of questions for you today.
And may I just say, well, first of all, thank you very much
to the three who for, for coming here today.
Uh, I, we we're far more well informed with regards to W
of ate, and it's very pleasing to, to hear perhaps
what you might call the uplift in, in the recent year of
what the focus is in this, what we consider,
and we should all consider is a very important area,
uh, area of your business.
Um, you know, we, we have been through a, um,
we have been through a history of the crown, um,
where it has shown, uh, to be very apologetic for, uh,
bad outcomes prior.
Um, so, so we want
to make sure we don't have bad outcomes moving forward.
And so the, the whole purpose of this briefing
that we're doing right now is to understand, uh,
but also to be able to take the information we along here
and see what else we can do with other, uh, conversations
that we can have as a committee.
So I appreciate, um,
all the information you brought for us today.
I, I thank you, uh, for your work
and also for your concentration
and the fact that there, you, there clearly w um, sees
that there needs to be more work done in the space,
hence there's other roles dedicated to it in, in itself.
So, uh, with that, um, hopefully in a,
if we do have the opportunity in a year's time
to come back together, you can be sitting there
and saying, well, we've nailed this, we've nailed that.
That'll be a great outcome as far as I Thank you. Okay.
I'm consultant.
